summary: Backend pytest suite executed against external preview URL. 21/24 tests passing. 3 real backend bugs found: (1) GET /api/ root endpoint returns 404 because @api.get('/') is declared AFTER app.include_router(api), so it is never registered. (2) POST /api/products returns 500 with `'ObjectId' object is not iterable` because motor's insert_one() mutates the dict by adding `_id` (BSON ObjectId) and the endpoint returns that mutated dict directly. (3) GET /api/payments/status/{session_id} returns 500 because the Emergent Stripe proxy (sk_test_emergent) responds with `resource_missing - No such checkout.session` immediately after the session is created successfully — likely an Emergent Stripe integration proxy issue, but the endpoint should also handle it gracefully (return 4xx/expose the status) instead of bubbling a 500. All other auth/products/cart/orders/checkout-create/chat endpoints work correctly. Latvian GPT-5.2 chat reply confirmed working.

critical.issue: 500 'ObjectId object is not iterable'. After `await db.products.insert_one(doc)`, motor injects `_id` (ObjectId) into the dict; the endpoint then `return doc`, which FastAPI cannot serialize. Fix: pop `_id` before returning, or return a fresh dict from `p.model_dump()`/use `{**doc, '_id': None}` style without mutating, or call `find_one` again with projection.

Returns 500 because Emergent Stripe proxy returns 404 'resource_missing' on the just-created session. Backend should catch the StripeError gracefully and return an actionable response (e.g., 502/404 with payment_status='pending'). Also flags a likely Emergent Stripe integration issue: POST create returns 200 with session_id, but immediate GET on the same id from the proxy is 404.

404. `@api.get('/')` is declared on line 664 — AFTER `app.include_router(api)` on line 649 — so the route is never registered on the app. Move route declaration above include_router, or call include_router after all routes are defined.

minor.issue: Always responds 200 even for new user creation; REST convention would be 201. Not breaking but worth aligning with PRD.
No rate limiting / brute-force lockout on /auth/login. Playbook recommends lockout after 5 failed attempts.

action_items: Fix POST /api/products: do not return the dict mutated by motor; pop '_id' or rebuild response from p.model_dump().
Fix GET /api/ root: move `@api.get('/')` declaration above `app.include_router(api)`.

Investigate Stripe payment status 500: wrap stripe_checkout.get_checkout_status in proper error handling and verify why the Emergent Stripe proxy returns 404 on the freshly created session_id (could be account isolation between create and read calls).
(Optional) Add brute-force lockout on /auth/login and use 201 on /auth/register.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single ~660 line file mixing auth, products, cart, orders, payments, chat, seeding, CORS — should be split into routers/services for maintainability.
`app.include_router(api)` is called BEFORE the final `@api.get('/')` is declared, causing that route to be silently dropped — anti-pattern, register all routes first.
`POST /api/products` returns the dict that motor mutated with `_id`; this is an MongoDB ObjectId leak risk pattern. Verify all other endpoints that return raw insert dicts.
CORS `allow_origins` uses '*' fallback while `allow_credentials=True` — browsers reject this combination; ensure FRONTEND_URL is always set in env.
No input validation on `category` in ProductCreate — accepts any string. Should restrict to {apgerbs, elektronika, smarzas}.
Cart pricing is computed server-side (good), but no stock decrement / overselling protection during checkout.
Payment status endpoint does not require auth — anyone with a session_id can poll status. Consider gating to the order owner.
JWT_SECRET stored in committed .env; ensure rotation in production.
test_credentials: admin@veikals.lv / Admin123! (admin), dynamically created TEST_*@example.com customers.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded products (6). Created ephemeral TEST_* users via /api/auth/register; created TEST_prod product (cleaned up in same test via DELETE).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers full API surface. After main agent fixes the 3 issues (root route ordering, product create ObjectId leak, payment status 500), re-run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml`. Auth uses cookies + Authorization Bearer; tests use Bearer extracted from cookie. Emergent Stripe sandbox quirk: create-session works but status-check returns 404 from proxy — main agent should investigate emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout flow.

rca of the issue.products_create_500: motor.insert_one mutates passed dict to add _id (ObjectId). Code does `await db.products.insert_one(doc); return doc` — FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize bson.ObjectId, raising 500. Repro: login as admin, POST /api/products with valid payload. Fix: `doc.pop('_id', None)` before return, OR `return p.model_dump(mode='json')`.
rca of the issue.root_404: `app.include_router(api)` runs at module load; routes added to the router AFTER that include call are not propagated to the app. The `@api.get('/')` decorator on line 664 runs after line 649's include_router. Fix: move include_router below all route declarations.
rca of the issue.payment_status_500: Stripe proxy (https://integrations.emergentagent.com/stripe/v1/) returns POST 200 with session_id=cs_test_... then GET on /checkout/sessions/{same_id} returns 404 'resource_missing'. Backend re-raises as 500. Either (a) emergentintegrations proxy isolation bug, or (b) session_id format mismatch. Backend should at minimum wrap and return a controlled response.
